commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 2 Chronicles 12:3 With twelve hundred chariots, and threescore thousand horsemen: and the people were without number that came with him out of Egypt; the Libyans, the Sukkiim, and the Ethiopians. Troglodites, who dwell in caverns, near the Red Sea. ) speaks of the city Suca, which in Hebrew means a tent, as here we read succiyim, (Haydock) "dwelling in tents "which some explain of the Arabs, who are called Scenites, on the same account. (Tirinus) People of this description, without any fixed abode, inhabited the Stony as well as the Desert Arabia, chap.
